title: pilosella strigosa
intro: Pilosella strigosa, also known as mouse-ear hawk's-beard, is a low-growing, flowering plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its saponins and flavonoids. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antiseptic properties. Pilosella strigosa can be prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used in herbal tinctures and salves for topical application.
